chief_complaint: A 45-year-old male with chronic stroke presented with difficulty performing bilateral tasks.
history: The patient has a history of left hemisphere stroke 5 years ago. He has been experiencing difficulty with bilateral hand and arm movements. He has no significant past medical history and is not taking any medications.
exam: Vital signs: BP 120/80, HR 70, RR 16. Physical examination revealed increased compensatory movements of proximal joints and asymmetries in bilateral hand and arm movements.
labs: ECG: Normal. Bloods: Normal. Imaging: Not mentioned.
